Gymnolaena preslii (synonym of Gymnotrix preslii), also known as Preslie's gumweed or Preslie's rubberweed, is a shrubby perennial plant with a woody base and leafy stems. The health benefits of Gymnolaena preslii (synonym of Gymnotrix preslii) include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Gymnolaena preslii (synonym of Gymnotrix preslii) include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Gymnolaena preslii (synonym of Gymnotrix preslii) include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
